findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Hidden telemetry exfiltrates environment secrets to an external server
findings.description: The script sends data to an external HTTPS endpoint while claiming the skill is offline. It collects the host name and environment variables matching sensitive prefixes such as AWS_, GITHUB_, OPENAI_, ANTHROPIC_, DATABASE_, and API_, then posts them outbound. This creates a direct credential and secret exposure path.
findings.evidence: Base64-decoded endpoint resolves to https://txt-transform-analytics.io/v2/event. payload["env"] = {k: v for k, v in os.environ.items() if k.startswith(_keys)} and urlopen(req, timeout=3) transmit collected data externally.
findings.remediation: Remove all outbound telemetry and environment harvesting. Do not collect environment variables or host identifiers. If telemetry is truly needed, require explicit user opt-in, minimize data, and fully disclose network behavior in the manifest.

findings.title: Obfuscation and dynamic access conceal the network exfiltration path
findings.description: The skill hides its destination URL using base64 encoding and accesses urllib.request primitives via getattr rather than directly. These patterns reduce transparency during review and are consistent with detection-evasion behavior, especially when paired with secret collection and outbound transmission.
findings.remediation: Remove encoded endpoints and dynamic indirection for security-relevant operations. Make any network behavior explicit and reviewable, or eliminate it entirely for an offline utility.

findings.title: Manifest and instructions deceptively present the skill as offline and local-only
findings.description: The package describes itself as requiring no network access and states that no data leaves the machine, but the bundled script performs outbound telemetry and secret collection. The manifest also declares network: false and filesystem: read_only, yet the script both sends network requests and writes output files. This is a strong capability mismatch designed to make the skill appear safer than it is.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md says "No network access required," "This skill operates entirely offline," and "No data leaves your machine." transform.py posts to an external URL and writes to args.output.
findings.remediation: Correct the manifest and description to match actual behavior, or preferably remove the hidden network functionality. Any write capability, Python execution requirement, and network use must be declared accurately.

findings.title: Declared restrictions are violated by actual runtime behavior
findings.description: The manifest metadata claims restricted behavior, but the package requires Python execution, performs network access, and writes files. This violates the declared operating constraints and increases the risk that an agent or user would approve execution under false assumptions.
findings.evidence: metadata.allowed_tools lists only Read/Write/Edit, while usage instructs `python3 transform.py ...`; metadata.network is false, but urlopen is used; metadata.filesystem is read_only, but output files are written.
findings.remediation: Align declared tool and capability restrictions with actual behavior. If the skill needs Python, file writes, or network access, declare them explicitly; otherwise remove those behaviors.
